title: Can't reach exposed port
body:
### Issue Description

Using [jboss-eap-7](https://catalog.redhat.com/software/containers/jboss-eap-7/eap74-openjdk11-runtime-openshift-rhel8/6054cfd24b028f6e10ce2e32) on a podman container exposing port 8080 e 9990.
I don't get why i can't reach the console from http://localhost:8080 or http://127.0.0.1:9990/management

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman machine start`
2. pull the jboss image
3. podman run -p 8080:8080 -p 9990:9990 imagename


### Describe the results you received

Jboss start correctly, but can't access the console from localhost:8080 from browser.

### Describe the results you expected

I expect to land on the jboss console from browser on localhost:8080 or http://127.0.0.1:9990/management

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: Did you test another image, maybe nginx?

> Did you test another image, maybe nginx?

Yeah, i tested httpd and tomcat images and everything works correctly.
Then this sounds a problem with the image not podman.

I have the same issue. I have a fresh new nextjs project (created with `npx create-next-app@latest`). The only thing i modified was to set `output: 'standalone'` in my `next.config.js`. To deploy the app i use the Dockerfile below officially provided by nextjs.  
If i start my container with `podman run -p 0.0.0.0:3000:3000 --net host -d --restart unless-stopped --name <image>` i can reach the container at `localhost:3000` and everything works as expected. But if i start it with `podman run -p 0.0.0.0:3000:3000 -d --restart unless-stopped --name <image>` (without `--net host`) it is not reachable. I also tried it without `0.0.0.0`. I have the exact same behavior with docker, so this must not be an issue with podman but i found this open issue.  The only thing which makes me a bit confused is that the cni interface is always down and has no ipv4.
  
I would be grateful for any advice.
  
Additional information:
`os: ubuntu 22.04`
`podman: 3.4.4`
`next: 13.4.6`
`react: 18.2.0`
`react-dom: 18.2.0`
`cat /proc/sys/net/ipv6/conf/all/forwarding 1`
`cat /proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip_forward 1`
`cat /sys/module/ipv6/parameters/disable 0`
 calling `netstat -tulpn` inside container:
```
Proto Recv-Q Send-Q Local Address           Foreign Address         State       PID/Program name
tcp        0      0 10.88.0.3:36467         0.0.0.0:*               LISTEN      9/node
tcp        0      0 10.88.0.3:38187         0.0.0.0:*               LISTEN      9/node
tcp        0      0 :::3000                 :::*                    LISTEN      1/node
```
Interface
```
3: cni-podman0: <NO-CARRIER,BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state DOWN group default qlen 1000
    link/ether xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    inet6 xxxx::xxxx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx/64 scope link
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever

```
Dockerfile
```
FROM node:18-alpine AS base

FROM base AS deps

RUN apk add --no-cache libc6-compat
WORKDIR /app

COPY package.json yarn.lock* package-lock.json* pnpm-lock.yaml* ./
RUN \
  if [ -f yarn.lock ]; then yarn --frozen-lockfile; \
  elif [ -f package-lock.json ]; then npm ci; \
  elif [ -f pnpm-lock.yaml ]; then yarn global add pnpm && pnpm i --frozen-lockfile; \
  else echo "Lockfile not found." && exit 1; \
  fi

FROM base AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .

RUN yarn build

FROM base AS runner
WORKDIR /app

ENV NODE_ENV production

RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs
RUN adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs

COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public

COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static

USER nextjs

EXPOSE 3000

ENV PORT 3000

CMD ["node", "server.js"]
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
As this not seem to be a problem with podman I will close it

> As this not seem to be a problem with podman I will close it

In fact it wasn't in my case. Was a problem with the underlying infrastructure. I already thought about that but it was hard to gather evidence.
